open: Decision needed: select plan format between a 4-week rotating weeknight menu (recipes, timings, master grocery list) or a single sample week (detailed 5 weeknight dinners with prep/cook timing and one weekend batch session).
Proposal: printable 4-week knife skills drill schedule with checklist.
Weekend projects added to plan: roast chicken, carnitas, ragu/bolognese; learnings and leftovers included.
Leftovers scaling guidelines: multipliers 1.5x or 2x; recommended container sizes; reheating tips.
Time-management plan: split practice sessions as per assistant guidance (weekend long 90–120 min, weekend medium 60–90 min, 3–5 short weekday drills of 10–30 min; weekly total 3–6 hours).
Printable 1-week schedule option offered: include specific recipes and exact time blocks that fit weeknight window.
Template options: ready-to-print weekly tracker or a simple phone note template.
15-minute recipe cards: prepare three printable cards tailored to Italian/Mexican preferences on request.
Capstone plan confirmation: two capstone dishes (roast chicken and carnitas) to map over six weeks.
Offer printable timed recipe cards for capstone dishes and a 6-week calendar mapping drills and recipes.

Open item: Recommendations for inexpensive, forgiving protein sources and cuts (e.g., chicken thighs, drumsticks, pork shoulder, beef chuck, beans, lentils, eggs, canned fish) that support technique-building on a budget.
